images.caption: Figure 1:We propose MoCam, a method that unifies novel view synthesis through structured denoising dynamics. Existing methods rely on static guidance that entangles geometry and appearance, often resulting in geometric collapse and visual artifacts. MoCam introduces structured denoising dynamics that guide generation from motion alignment to appearance refinement, producing coherent and photorealistic results.

Figure 2:Overview of the MoCam Framework.Given a source videoxsrcx^{\text{src}}(or a single image repeated for N frames), a geometrically-aligned but imperfect scaffold videoxrentgtx^{\text{tgt}}_{\text{ren}}is first rendered along the target trajectoryψtgt\psi^{\text{tgt}}.
After encoding these conditions into latent space, the model processes the initial noisez0z_{0}via the proposed structured denoising dynamics. Specifically, in the early stage, the denosing is guided by the scaffold conditioncrenc^{\text{ren}}to establish geometrically-aligned noisezT​_​s​w​i​t​c​hz_{T\_switch}.
Subsequently, the signal switches to the original source conditioncsrcc^{\text{src}}to obtain the clean latentzt​g​tz^{tgt}with refined appearance and decode it as the target videoxt​g​tx^{tgt}.
This temporal decoupling of conditions prevents the propagation of scaffold errors, enabling stable and photorealistic synthesis.

Figure 5:Qualitative results from in-the-wild videos. The first example illustrates an ’orbit-to-left’ trajectory, while the second example demonstrates a camera motion that initially moves to the top-left with zoom-in, followed by a transition to the bottom-right with a corresponding zoom-out.

Table 2:Quantitative 4D re-camera comparisons on the OpenVid dataset. BC: Background Consistency, SC: Subject Consistency, IQ: Imaging Quality, RotErr: Rotation Error, TransErr: Translation Error. Cells highlighted inredandyellowdenote the best and second-best performance.
Figure 6:Quantitative results of VBench metrics on various motion magnitudes.
Figure 7:Qualitative results on various motion scales. The models are inferred under camera trajectories with three different scales of orbit degree.
Figure 9:Ablation results on structured denoising dynamics.
